skill.name: azure-observability
skill.description: Guidance for Azure observability with Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics, alerts, workbooks, KQL, and SDK references.
skill.summary: Use Azure Monitor and related services to query logs, inspect metrics, and plan observability workflows.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many command, network, environment, and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found documentation examples rather than executable skill code. The skill contains Azure CLI examples, Azure SDK snippets, KQL samples, Microsoft Learn links, and normal environment-variable guidance for telemetry configuration. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, hidden execution logic, or confirmed malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Code Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. The external command findings are Markdown references to Azure MCP commands, Azure CLI examples, package installation commands, SDK imports, and KQL snippets. They are not hidden executable code in the skill package, but users should review commands before running them against an Azure tenant.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced code blocks, inline command names, package dependencies, or documentation snippets. No script file, dynamic command construction, or automatic execution path was found.

low_findings.title: Azure URLs Are Documentation and Endpoint Templates
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. The network findings are Microsoft Learn links and an Azure Monitor ingestion endpoint template. They do not send data, open sockets, or direct credentials to a third-party service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links or an Azure service endpoint pattern. There is no fetch, curl, webhook, or exfiltration logic associated with them.

low_findings.title: Telemetry Connection String Read From Environment
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive with minor handling caution. The TypeScript SDK example reads APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING from process.env for Azure Monitor setup. This is normal secret handling and the snippet does not log, transmit, or expose the value outside Azure telemetry configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable is a documented Application Insights configuration path. It is still a secret-bearing value, so users should avoid printing it or committing it.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Match Non-Crypto Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. The weak cryptography findings point to the skill description and KQL ordering text, not cryptographic functions, hash algorithms, encryption settings, or certificate handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain words such as description or descending sort syntax. No MD5, SHA1, DES, encryption API, or crypto implementation is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. The critical heuristic combined documentation command examples, Azure URLs, and environment-variable examples. Manual review found no obfuscation, prompt injection, credential harvesting, hidden network calls, or malicious control flow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The component findings are individually explainable as legitimate Azure observability documentation. A broad search found no suspicious override instructions or semantic exfiltration pattern.

content.value_statement: Azure observability work often spans metrics, logs, traces, alerts, and dashboards. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for Azure Monitor, KQL, Application Insights, and related SDKs.
actual_capabilities: Maps Azure observability services to common monitoring tasks.
Provides Azure MCP tool names and Azure CLI fallback examples.
Includes KQL examples for recent errors, request performance, and CPU usage.
Summarizes monitoring strategy across application, infrastructure, security, and cost signals.
Links condensed SDK references for OpenTelemetry, Monitor Query, ingestion, and Application Insights management.
limitations: It does not authenticate to Azure or create resources by itself.
Examples require valid Azure permissions, workspace identifiers, and resource identifiers.
SDK references are condensed and do not replace full Microsoft documentation.
KQL samples must be adapted to each workspace schema and retention policy.

use_cases.title: Investigate Production Errors
use_cases.description: Use KQL and Application Insights guidance to inspect recent exceptions, failed requests, and slow operations.

use_cases.title: Plan Azure Monitoring Coverage
use_cases.description: Select the right Azure service for metrics, traces, logs, alerts, workbooks, and cost signals.

use_cases.title: Add Telemetry to Applications
use_cases.description: Use SDK quick references to configure OpenTelemetry, query logs, or ingest custom logs.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Monitoring Services
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose Azure observability services for a web application. Include metrics, logs, traces, alerts, and dashboards.

prompt_templates.title: Write a KQL Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create KQL queries to investigate recent errors and slow requests in Application Insights for the last hour.

prompt_templates.title: Review Alert Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Azure monitoring plan and suggest alert rules for availability, latency, dependency failures, and infrastructure saturation.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Observability Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Azure observability workflow for multiple services using Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Application Insights, OpenTelemetry, and custom log ingestion.

output_examples.input: I need to investigate high latency in an Azure-hosted API.
output: A focused investigation path using Application Insights requests, dependency timing, and Azure Monitor metrics.
Suggested KQL query goals for average duration, request counts, and slow endpoint ranking.
Follow-up checks for sampling, missing telemetry, and infrastructure saturation.

output_examples.input: I want to add monitoring to a Node.js service running in Azure.
output: A setup outline using Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry for TypeScript.
Guidance to store the Application Insights connection string in an environment variable.
Best practices for early initialization, sampling, offline storage, and graceful shutdown.

output_examples.input: My team needs an observability baseline for a new Azure workload.
output: A service map covering Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics, alerts, and workbooks.
Recommended signals for application errors, response time, dependencies, CPU, audit logs, and costs.
Practical next steps for dashboards, alert routing, retention, and query validation.

best_practices: Use managed identity or Azure identity libraries instead of hardcoded secrets.
Limit KQL result sizes and project only the fields needed for investigation.
Validate alert thresholds against normal workload behavior before enabling paging.
anti_patterns: Do not paste connection strings, tokens, or tenant secrets into prompts or logs.
Do not run broad log queries across long time ranges without cost and performance checks.
Do not rely on metrics alone when traces, dependencies, and logs are needed.

faq.question: Does this skill run Azure commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users or enabled tools decide whether to run commands.

faq.question: Which Azure services does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics, alerts, workbooks, and related SDKs.

faq.question: Can it help write KQL queries?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes common KQL patterns for errors, request performance, and resource usage.

faq.question: Does it require Azure MCP?
faq.answer: Azure MCP is preferred for tool-backed workflows, but the skill also includes Azure CLI references.

faq.question: Can it configure OpenTelemetry?
faq.answer: It provides condensed Python and TypeScript setup references for Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found documentation examples and no confirmed malicious behavior.
